payload.answer: Knowledge graphs are data structures that represent information as a network of entities (nodes) connected by relationships (edges). Each node and edge can have attributes (labels/types). They support flexible, graph-based queries and are used to model interconnected data such as social networks, semantic webs, and knowledge bases. Common vocabularies include RDF triples (subject, predicate, object) and property graphs. If you want, I can tailor an introductory reading plan or demonstration queries.

entity_resolution.reason: Initial clarification of core concepts for future linking and memory preservation.

memory_cards.statement: Knowledge graphs model entities and their relationships as a graph. Core components include nodes (entities), edges (relationships), and labels/types for both. They enable querying interconnected data more naturally than tabular representations.

details: Memory entry created for core knowledge graph concepts.
